headlineSummary: OpenAI and Anthropic both skew negative with a close tie and high-confidence, high-support evidence, while Google Gemini skews positive on strong praise for usefulness and fit [E3][E4][E6][E8].

ranking.summary: OpenAI skews negative on trust, pricing, and business-model concerns, with its negative tone closely tied to Anthropic and supported by high-confidence evidence [E3][E5][E6][E10][E11].
Anthropic also skews negative, with repeated concerns about trust, pricing, and competitive position, and it is in a close tie with OpenAI [E1][E3][E4][E5][E6][E7][E8][E10][E11].
Google Gemini skews positive, driven by comments calling it indispensable, great for specific tasks, and competitively strong against alternatives [E2][E4][E6][E8].

evidence.excerpt: I get the whole trope thing and maybe I'm just an old man but I still am kinda impressed when Claude sh*ts out this type of UI 100 times faster than I ever could. It might also be that I never could have made UI even of this quality before AI.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Claude and says they are impressed by its speed and output quality for UI generation.

evidence.excerpt: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Google Gemini in a factual announcement about Apple's architecture; it does not express praise or criticism.

evidence.excerpt: If I can't even trust the results given by ChatGPT and Claude at their highest level of reasoning in my daily life and work, would I be willing to use Siri AI to handle the important scenarios depicted in the livestream?

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says they cannot trust ChatGPT's results, which is a direct negative statement about OpenAI's system.
The commenter explicitly says they cannot trust Claude's results, a direct negative statement about Anthropic's system.

evidence.excerpt: I've been a paid subscriber to Claude for a couple of years, but lately I've been reaching for the free Gemini app on my Android Pixel 9 because it's so good at doing searches as part of its answers. The model feels fresh and up to date.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Gemini as good at search-integrated answers and says it feels fresh and up to date.
The commenter contrasts years of paid Claude use with now preferring Gemini for answer quality and freshness, implying Claude is weaker on those dimensions.

evidence.excerpt: Why would you pay for ChatGPT, or even tolerate its inevitably increasingly desperate ad placements? Assume Google will have similar tools in their phones, and Google search will continue to have the offering it does. In short, where is the evidence that once Apple's tech exists, consumer AI is worth, to Anthropic or OpenAI, anything noticeably more than that $1B a year?

annotations.rationale: The commenter questions paying for ChatGPT and frames OpenAI's consumer AI economics as weak once built-in alternatives arrive.
The commenter explicitly questions whether consumer AI is worth much to Anthropic, implying weak business prospects.

evidence.excerpt: It always seemed very natural to me that AI will move “down the stack”, where Open AI and Anthropic don’t really have a foot in the door. Who makes consumer devices? Google Who makes operating systems? Google Who makes browsers? Google Who makes the world’s most popular websites? Google By the time 90% of average internet users get to chatgpt.com or whatever, they already went through several Google chokepoints, each layer is one more place Google can answer their questions. And that’s not even getting into the chips, the data centers, the data, the talent, the consumer apps, the enterprise apps, the cloud platform, the brand, and of course the biggest cash printing machine in human history. You would honestly have to be insane to bet against G.

annotations.rationale: The comment says OpenAI does not really have a foot in the door as AI moves down the stack, indicating a negative view of its competitive position.
The comment explicitly says Anthropic does not really have a foot in the door, a direct criticism of its competitive position.
In AI-market context, the commenter lists Google's platform advantages and concludes it would be insane to bet against Google, expressing a very strong positive view of Google's AI position.

evidence.excerpt: I want to like this but it reads like Claude output, how much scrutiny did this get for accuracy?

annotations.rationale: The commenter uses 'Claude output' as a shorthand for content whose accuracy is doubtful, signaling a negative trust judgment.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini has been indispensable for helping me move from Windows to Linux. I'm reasonably proficient, but moving to a brand-new OS brought so many random questions and weird edge cases that I never would have had the confidence, patience, or time to tackle it alone, even with pretty strong Google fu. It's been so nice to have instant access to answers for my specific problems, without judgment or having to wait on a reply. At the same time, I moved from Chrome back to Firefox, and Gemini was great at finding equivalents for my most-used extensions -- and, when none existed, to write my own. It's also been really useful for customization/"ricing". More recently, I got into Quod Libet as a primary music library manager, and both Gemini and Claude have been fantastic at helping me build custom plugins that make it do exactly what I want it to.

annotations.rationale: The commenter calls Gemini indispensable, says it was great, and credits it with answering specific problems and helping build tools.
The commenter says Claude has been fantastic at helping build custom plugins, which is clear praise.

evidence.excerpt: then I’ve noticed models like GPT 5.5 being clearly better at working out a solid solution on their own.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says GPT 5.5 is clearly better at independently finding solid solutions.

evidence.excerpt: I can't come up with a use case where I can rationally spend ~31 times what Opus costs to use GPT 5.5 Pro, and I won't be doing any more benchmarking with it. Given how much token costs are becoming an issue people talk about, the fact that there are models that cost dramatically less than the big American providers is going to be an issue for Anthropic and OpenAI.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says GPT 5.5 Pro is irrationally expensive relative to alternatives and rejects further use because of cost.
The commenter says much cheaper models will be an issue for Anthropic, indicating a negative view of Anthropic's pricing competitiveness.

evidence.excerpt: It is increasingly look like OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX (xAI) are going to burst their own AI bubble by going public. Their businesses aren't ready for that kind of quarter-by-quarter grinding scrutiny. It is going to be bad when their lockup periods end.

annotations.rationale: The commenter predicts OpenAI will burst its AI bubble by going public and says the business is not ready, which is strongly negative.
The commenter explicitly groups Anthropic among firms that will burst their AI bubble and are not ready for public-market scrutiny.
